title: Outbound call logging failure resets UI while the SIP call may still be active
reasoning: The SIP invite is sent before the call log is created. If api.createCallLog fails after inviter.invite() succeeds, the catch block treats the whole call as failed and clears callState/currentCall even though the outbound SIP session may still be ringing or later connect. That makes the UI lose track of an in-progress call and reports a false call failure for a non-call-control error.
reproduction: Mock inviter.invite() to resolve and api.createCallLog() to reject. makeCall() will set the call to ringing, then immediately reset it to idle while sessionRef still points at the inviter.
recommendation: Separate SIP call setup errors from call-log persistence errors. Keep the call UI/session state after a successful invite, and handle logging failure independently, for example by logging/reporting it without calling onCallFailed or clearing currentCall.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for this feature, and there is no regression coverage for partial failure after a successful SIP invite.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a hook test that stubs Inviter.invite to resolve and api.createCallLog to reject, then asserts callState/currentCall remain in the outbound call state and onCallFailed is not invoked for the logging failure.
minimumFixScope: Update src/hooks/useSip.js makeCall so call logging is isolated from SIP invite control flow.
